steps.title: Identify the recurring action
steps.content: The key signal is “Each year in the fall”—this phrase marks this as a habitual, recurring action.

steps.title: Match the tense to the context
steps.content:
Simple present tense expresses habitual or recurring actions. “Fly” (D) correctly uses present tense for an action that happens every year.

The other three options are all past tense forms, which express a completed action rather than a recurring event.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
